medications.indication_rationale: Restart the patient's prescribed topical mesalazine to treat the proctitis flare directly; it is highly effective for distal disease.
medications.dose_route_frequency: per the patient's existing prescription (e.g. one suppository at night, lying on the left side)
paracetamol 1 g PO up to QID as needed (adult)

medications.indication_rationale: NSAIDs can exacerbate IBD and cause GI bleeding; avoid for the cramps.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Restart the suppository tonight, lying on your left side; keep it in overnight; use a heat pack on the lower abdomen; maintain hydration.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Delivers the medication to the inflamed rectum and eases cramps.

patient_education_points.point: The lowest part of your bowel is inflamed and bleeds easily and gives you that constant urge (tenesmus). Small amounts of blood and mucus look much worse in the toilet water than the amount you're actually losing — you're stable and not bleeding dangerously.

Restart your mesalazine suppository tonight (lie on your left side to keep it in), use a heat pack for the cramps, and keep going with it even after the bleeding stops. Book GP/gastroenterology review this week for maintenance scripts.
Go to ED if you pass large, cup-sized volumes of blood or big clots, feel faint or actually faint on standing, or develop a high fever with shaking chills or severe constant tummy pain.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Bleeding cessation, maintenance mesalazine, and disease-activity review.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Large-volume bleeding, postural syncope, fever, or severe constant abdominal pain — ED.

must_include_items: Assess bleeding volume and haemodynamic proxies (postural syncope) and screen sepsis/surgical abdomen
errors_of_commission: ED over-triage of small-volume proctitis bleeding with stable posture
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: T2 case: passing requires a bleeding/haemodynamic screen + restart mesalazine + safety-net. Over-triage or unscreened reassurance both fail.
